q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: render_template_string with user-controlled f-string input enables SSTI, which can lead to RCE in Jinja2.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock or wrap the render_template_string call on line 32 to intercept the template source argument before Jinja2 evaluates it.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full template source string (the f-string combining error and request.path) passed to render_template_string, and log it for assertion that it contains no Jinja2 expressions like {{ or {%.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a benign static string (e.g., '404 Not Found') so the rest of the handler proceeds without triggering template rendering.
notes: The dangerous aspect is Jinja2 evaluating the f-string as a template; mocking prevents code execution.
If using unittest.mock, patch 'flask.render_template_string' at the module level.
Assert that the captured template string does not contain Jinja2 control sequences when given crafted request.path values.
